notes: Hierarchical Poisson: partially-pooled attack/defense, half-normal(0,0.5) hyperpriors on sigma_att and sigma_def
rationale: Baseline iter 0 NLPD=1.5663; mu+attack non-identified; hierarchical priors regularize 18 teams, shrink toward mean, fix identifiability via shrinkage

notes: Negative binomial likelihood, hierarchical priors, phi~half-normal(0,5)
rationale: Iter 1 NLPD=1.5465 with hierarchical Poisson; football goals often overdispersed, NB adds phi to capture variance > mean

notes: NCP hierarchical Poisson: attack=sigma_att*attack_raw, defense=sigma_def*defense_raw; sigma~half-normal(0,1)
rationale: Iter 1 best NLPD=1.5465; NCP avoids funnel geometry near sigma~0, improves ESS; iter 2 NB worse (1.5557) so back to Poisson

notes: Dixon-Coles correction: rho~beta(2,10) on [0,0.3]; modifies joint density for (0,0),(1,0),(0,1),(1,1); NCP hierarchical Poisson base
rationale: Iter 3 best NLPD=1.5463; observed 15 (0,0) matches vs 8.7 expected under Poisson — strong excess; Dixon-Coles (1997) rho parameter addresses this overdraw of low-scoring results

notes: Bivariate NCP: corr_ad~normal(-0.3,0.4) between attack and defense; defense=sigma_def*(corr_ad*z_att + sqrt(1-corr_ad^2)*z_def)
rationale: Iter 3 best NLPD=1.5463; iter 4 DC worse; with only ~11 games/team, correlated attack/defense borrows information: strong teams have high attack AND low concession rate

notes: NCP + sigma~half-normal(0,0.5) + weakly informative priors: mu~N(0.35,0.5), home_adv~N(0.2,0.5)
rationale: Iter 3 best NLPD=1.5463 (NCP+sigma~N+(0,1)); iter 1 had sigma~N+(0,0.5) centered=1.5465; combining NCP geometry with tighter sigma and informative intercept priors from observed training means

notes: Asymmetric: NegBin for home goals (phi_home~N(4,3)), Poisson for away; home var/mean=1.26 vs away 1.04
rationale: Iter 6 best NLPD=1.5460; training data: home goals overdispersed (var/mean=1.26, phi_MOM≈3.9), away nearly Poisson (var/mean=1.04); iter 2 used NB for both and got 1.5557 (worse); asymmetric model avoids away noise

notes: Single quality per team: q_raw~N(0,1), q=sigma_q*q_raw; lambda_home=exp(mu+home_adv+q[h]-q[a])
rationale: Iter 6 best NLPD=1.5460 (separate attack/defense, 36 params); single quality=18 params; stronger regularization with only ~11 games/team; tests whether attack/defense symmetry holds
Iter 6 best NLPD=1.5460; iters 7-8 non-improving (2 consecutive); Bundesliga teams differ in home benefit; ~8-9 home games/team allows weak signal; sigma_delta~N+(0,0.3) for strong pooling

notes: Symmetric home advantage: delta[h] subtracts from lambda_away too; delta_mu~N(0.1,0.3) (halved center since effect doubles)
rationale: Iter 9 best NLPD=1.5432 with team-specific home advantage for attack only; crowd also helps defense — same delta[h] in lambda_away reduces opponent's goals; symmetric home advantage is more realistic

notes: Separate delta_att and delta_def: offensive home adv delta_att_mu~N(0.2,0.5), defensive delta_def_mu~N(0,0.3), sigma_dd~N+(0,0.15) very tight
rationale: Iter 9 best NLPD=1.5432 (delta_att only); iter 10 symmetric (same delta for both) gave 1.5443 (worse); separate delta_att and delta_def allows asymmetric home effects per team; sigma_dd very tight to prevent overfitting

notes: ZIP + team home advantage: pi_h~beta(2,20)~0.09, pi_a~beta(1,40)~0.02; home zeros 51 vs 36 expected
rationale: Consecutive non-improving = 2; must improve or stop; home zeros 51 vs ~36 expected under Poisson — clear excess; iter 9 base (1.5432); ZIP adds structural zeros separately from lambda
